heading: Establishment of World Trade Center Health Program
label: 300mm Establishment of World Trade Center Health Program
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (f)(2)(E). substituted “subsection (a) or (c) of ” for “”.Pub. L. 117–328section 300mm–51 of this titlesection 300mm–51(a) of this title2015—Subsecs. (i) to (k). added subsecs. (i) to (k).Pub. L. 114–113

children.text: medical monitoring and treatment benefits to eligible emergency responders and recovery and cleanup workers (including those who are Federal employees) who responded to the , terrorist attacks; andSeptember 11, 2001

initial health evaluation, monitoring, and treatment benefits to residents and other building occupants and area workers in New York City who were directly impacted and adversely affected by such attacks.

children.heading: Medical monitoring for responders
children.label: 1 Medical monitoring for responders
children.text: Medical monitoring under , including clinical examinations and long-term health monitoring and analysis for enrolled WTC responders who were likely to have been exposed to airborne toxins that were released, or to other hazards, as a result of the , terrorist attacks.section 300mm–21 of this titleSeptember 11, 2001

children.heading: Initial health evaluation for survivors
children.label: 2 Initial health evaluation for survivors
children.text: An initial health evaluation under , including an evaluation to determine eligibility for followup monitoring and treatment.section 300mm–31 of this title

children.heading: Followup monitoring and treatment for WTC-related health conditions for responders and survivors
children.label: 3 Followup monitoring and treatment for WTC-related health conditions for responders and survivors
children.text: Provision under sections 300mm–22, 300mm–32, and 300mm–33 of this title of followup monitoring and treatment and payment, subject to the provisions of subsection (d), for all medically necessary health and mental health care expenses of an individual with respect to a WTC-related health condition (including necessary prescription drugs).

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: 4 Outreach
children.text: Establishment under of an education and outreach program to potentially eligible individuals concerning the benefits under this subchapter.section 300mm–2 of this title

children.heading: Clinical data collection and analysis
children.label: 5 Clinical data collection and analysis
children.text: Collection and analysis under of health and mental health data relating to individuals receiving monitoring or treatment benefits in a uniform manner in collaboration with the collection of epidemiological data under .section 300mm–3 of this titlesection 300mm–52 of this title

children.heading: Research on health conditions
children.label: 6 Research on health conditions
children.text: Establishment under part C of a research program on health conditions resulting from the , terrorist attacks.September 11, 2001

children.heading: No cost sharing
children.label: c No cost sharing
children.text: Monitoring and treatment benefits and initial health evaluation benefits are provided under part B without any deductibles, copayments, or other cost sharing to an enrolled WTC responder or certified-eligible WTC survivor. Initial health evaluation benefits are provided under part B without any deductibles, copayments, or other cost sharing to a screening-eligible WTC survivor.

children.heading: Preventing fraud and unreasonable administrative costs
children.label: d Preventing fraud and unreasonable administrative costs

children.heading: Fraud
children.label: 1 Fraud
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall develop and implement a program to review the WTC Program’s health care expenditures to detect fraudulent or duplicate billing and payment for inappropriate services. This subchapter is a Federal health care program (as defined in ) and is a health plan (as defined in ) for purposes of applying sections 1320a–7 through 1320a–7e of this title.section 1320a–7b(f) of this titlesection 1320a–7c(c) of this title

children.heading: Unreasonable administrative costs
children.label: 2 Unreasonable administrative costs
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall develop and implement a program to review the WTC Program for unreasonable administrative costs, including with respect to infrastructure, administration, and claims processing.
adherence to monitoring and treatment protocols;
appropriate diagnostic and treatment referrals for participants;
prompt communication of test results to participants; and
such other elements as the Administrator specifies in consultation with the Clinical Centers of Excellence.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the end of each fiscal year in which the WTC Program is in operation, the WTC Program Administrator shall submit an annual report to the Congress on the operations of this subchapter for such fiscal year and for the entire period of operation of the program.

children.label: i
children.text: on the number of individuals who applied for certification under part B and the number of such individuals who were so certified;

children.label: ii
children.text: of the individuals who were certified, on the number who received monitoring under the program and the number of such individuals who received medical treatment under the program;

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to individuals so certified who received such treatment, on the WTC-related health conditions for which they were treated; and

children.label: iv
children.text: on the projected number of individuals who will be certified under part B in the succeeding fiscal year and the succeeding 10-year period.

children.heading: Monitoring, initial health evaluation, and treatment costs
children.label: B Monitoring, initial health evaluation, and treatment costs
i
children.text: information on the costs of monitoring and initial health evaluation and the costs of treatment and on the estimated costs of such monitoring, evaluation, and treatment in the succeeding fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an estimate of the cost of medical treatment for WTC-related health conditions that have been paid for or reimbursed by workers’ compensation, by public or private health plans, or by New York City under .section 300mm–41 of this title

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: C Administrative costs
children.text: Information on the cost of administering the program, including costs of program support, data collection and analysis, and research conducted under the program.

children.label: i
children.text: the performance of the program in providing timely evaluation of and treatment to eligible individuals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a list of the Clinical Centers of Excellence and other providers that are participating in the program.

children.heading: Scientific reports
children.label: E Scientific reports
children.text: A summary of the findings of any new scientific reports or studies on the health effects associated with exposure described in , including the findings of research conducted under subsection (a) or (c) of .section 300mm–5(1) of this titlesection 300mm–51 of this title

children.heading: Advisory Committee recommendations
children.label: F Advisory Committee recommendations
children.text: A list of recommendations by the WTC Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee on additional WTC Program eligibility criteria and on additional WTC-related health conditions and the action of the WTC Program Administrator concerning each such recommendation.

children.heading: Firefighters and related personnel
children.label: A Firefighters and related personnel
children.text: The benefits provided for enrolled WTC responders described in .section 300mm–21(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Other WTC responders
children.label: B Other WTC responders
children.text: The benefits provided for enrolled WTC responders not described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: WTC survivors
children.label: C WTC survivors
children.text: The benefits provided for screening-eligible WTC survivors and certified-eligible WTC survivors in .section 300mm–31(a) of this title

children.heading: Notification to Congress upon reaching 80 percent of eligibility numerical limits
children.label: g Notification to Congress upon reaching 80 percent of eligibility numerical limits

children.text: When the number of enrollments of WTC responders subject to the limit established under has reached 80 percent of such limit.section 300mm–21(a)(4) of this title
When the number of certifications for certified-eligible WTC survivors subject to the limit established under has reached 80 percent of such limit.section 300mm–31(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: h Consultation
children.text: The WTC Program Administrator shall engage in ongoing outreach and consultation with relevant stakeholders, including the WTC Health Program Steering Committees and the Advisory Committee under , regarding the implementation and improvement of programs under this subchapter.section 300mm–1 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The quality assurance program developed and implemented under subsection (e).

children.label: B
children.text: The procedures for providing certifications of coverage of conditions as WTC-related health conditions for enrolled WTC responders under and for screening-eligible WTC survivors and certified-eligible WTC survivors under such section as applied under .section 300mm–22(b)(2)(B)(iii) of this titlesection 300mm–32(a) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Any action under the WTC Program to ensure appropriate payment (including the avoidance of improper payments), including determining the extent to which individuals enrolled in the WTC Program are eligible for workers compensation or sources of health coverage, ascertaining the liability of such compensation or sources of health coverage, and making recommendations for ensuring effective and efficient coordination of benefits for individuals enrolled in the WTC Program that does not place an undue burden on such individuals.

children.label: A
children.text: consult the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate on the objectives in assessing the WTC Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: prepare and submit to such Committees a report that assesses the WTC Program for the applicable reporting period, including the objectives described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: j Regulations
children.text: The WTC Program Administrator is authorized to promulgate such regulations as the Administrator determines necessary to administer this subchapter.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: k Termination
children.text: The WTC Program shall terminate on .October 1, 2090
